records.ety: [AS. lust, lust, pleasure, longing; akin to OS., D., G., & Sw. lust, Dan. & Icel. lyst, Goth lustus, and perh. tom Skr. lush to desire, or to E. loose. Cf. List to please, Listless.]

quotes: For little lust had she to talk of aught.
My lust to devotion is little.

defs: Longing desire; eagerness to possess or enjoy; -- in a had sense; as, the lust of gain.
Licentious craving; a strong sexual appetite.

records.ety: [AS. lystan. See Lust, n., and cf. List to choose.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Lusted; p. pr. & vb. n. Lusting.]

quotes: In the water vessel he it cast When that him luste.
notes: ☞ In earlier usage lust was impersonal. In the water vessel he it cast When that him luste. Chaucer. [1913 Webster]

defs: To have an eager, passionate, and especially an inordinate or sinful desire, as for the gratification of the sexual appetite or of covetousness; -- often with after.
quotes: Whosoever looketh on a woman to lust after her, hath committed adultery with her already in his heart.
The spirit that dwelleth in us lusteth to envy.
